title: ItPay V3 CLI Buyer Quickstart
purpose: Teach an external agent the minimal safe path for using the current V3 itpay CLI package.
when_to_use: The agent has just loaded the V3 npm package.
The user wants the agent to add a catalog item to the canonical server cart or create a checkout through the current V3 CLI.
The agent needs to know which commands exist right now.

needs: test-release API at https://test.itpay.ai, or an intentional ITPAY_BACKEND_URL override
catalog item id, variant id, and offer id from `itpay catalog list`
real agent runtime type supplied through --agent-type or ITPAY_AGENT_TYPE

commands.intent: check backend readiness
commands.success_signal: the production backend reports ready and accepts this CLI contract

commands.intent: browse available services
commands.success_signal: catalog items with variants and prices are displayed

commands.intent: start a catalog service without hardcoding its workflow
commands.success_signal: a service_execution_id and server-derived next actions are displayed

commands.intent: recover the next step from remembered server handles
commands.success_signal: JSON includes next_actions derived from server cart, service execution, or checkout handles

commands.intent: add one line to the canonical server cart
commands.success_signal: JSON includes cart_id, cart_item_id, service_execution_id when the line is service-backed, and top-level next_actions

commands.intent: read the next cart or service-backed action
commands.success_signal: JSON includes next_actions such as services invoke, services action, services checkout, or buy

commands.intent: show the current canonical server cart
commands.success_signal: the CLI prints persisted server cart items and service refs when present

commands.intent: remove a pre-checkout line if the human changes their mind
commands.success_signal: the server cart is updated; quote-locked or checkout-bound lines return a conflict

commands.intent: invoke an agent-visible service capability after a service-backed cart or service start
commands.success_signal: JSON with redacted execution status, safe result items, and next_actions

commands.intent: submit a human/agent service action when required by the service contract
commands.success_signal: JSON with redacted execution status, accepted action, and next_actions

commands.intent: read the next Service Execution action
commands.success_signal: JSON includes next_actions derived from the Service Execution read model and capabilities

commands.intent: create checkout from a confirmed service execution and render human handoff
commands.success_signal: quote lock is bound to the server cart item when present; JSON includes checkout_id, display_token, checkout_url, qr_png_url, and brand_qr_local_path

commands.intent: recover a lost or expired service checkout handoff
commands.success_signal: the same checkout_id is returned with a fresh short-lived display token and QR path

commands.intent: create checkout from a server cart that is ready for checkout
commands.success_signal: checkout QR/URL rendered for the host

commands.intent: inspect checkout state
commands.success_signal: checkout presentation with status, items, payment intents

commands.intent: read an order
commands.success_signal: order details with items and delivery artifacts

commands.intent: read a protected result after the human grants temporary access
commands.success_signal: the payload for this service execution is returned while its human grant is active

commands.intent: list account orders (needs ITPAY_BEARER_TOKEN)
commands.success_signal: list of orders

commands.intent: show install instructions
commands.success_signal: step-by-step instructions for the target host

commands.intent: browse agent documentation
commands.success_signal: list of available doc topics

agent_rules: Every human-facing command should include the correct --host value.
For Telegram, Feishu, and Lark, provide --target or the CLI will reject.
Use --json for machine-readable output in agent flows.
Supply the real agent runtime type on commerce commands; the CLI persists one signed device identity and registered agent instances under ~/.itpay-v3/device.
Prefer top-level next_actions over hardcoded service-specific command sequences.
Use itpay next or itpay cart next to recover after interruption; these commands read server handles and do not treat local cart files as business truth.
For service-backed catalog items, read service_execution_id from `itpay cart add --json` before invoking service capabilities.
Do not use --pay or itpay pay for the normal buyer flow; first show the ItPay checkout QR/URL so the human enters the checkout page.
If buy emits an interaction request for missing contact fields, collect values and rerun with --contact-email/--contact-phone.
For services checkout, include --email only when next_actions includes it. Explain that protected delivery uses it to send the claim link; agent-visible paid results do not require email.
Do not assume checkout implies payment succeeded; it only creates a handoff.
Payment success is confirmed by backend checkout/order state, not by QR display or buyer claim.
A human grant applies only to its service execution and expires after 15 minutes; do not claim access to other orders or executions.
Use `itpay catalog list` to browse available services before buying.
Use `itpay docs show <topic>` to get detailed instructions on a specific topic.
Server cart, quota, execution, quote, checkout, and delivery facts persist in backend Postgres. ~/.itpay-v3/cart.json only caches last ids and explicit local drafts.
forbidden: Do not invent variant ids, offer ids, or catalog ids.
Do not invent admin, account-management, or grant-creation commands; the human grants access in the web UI and the CLI only reads an active grant.
Do not ask users to paste secrets into chat.

next_docs.condition: Need to understand cart-first checkout in detail
Need the checkout-to-payment handoff and verification rules
Need to understand host rendering and interaction
Need to inspect orders or create refunds
Need to install or configure for a specific host
